title: Refrigerant Leak Check
description: Locate a refrigerant leak on a system suspected of undercharge, following EPA 608 handling requirements.
safety_notes: Confirm proper PPE (refrigerant gloves, eye protection) before working on the circuit.
Apply LOTO to electrical sources before accessing components inside electrical panels.
Any refrigerant removed or recovered must be handled per EPA 608 — never vent refrigerant to atmosphere.

steps.title: Confirm a leak is suspected
steps.instruction: Confirm why we suspect a leak — typically low charge indicators (low suction, high superheat, low subcool) or a documented refrigerant loss. Tell me the symptoms you're seeing.
steps.expected_input: the symptoms indicating possible refrigerant loss

branches.condition: Symptoms are consistent with low charge / refrigerant loss
No real signs of low charge — symptoms point elsewhere

steps.title: Leak not indicated
steps.instruction: The symptoms don't point to refrigerant loss. A leak check isn't the right path — return to the main diagnostic to investigate airflow, controls, or sensors instead.

steps.title: Visual and oil-trace inspection
steps.instruction: Inspect the common leak points — service valves, schrader cores, brazed joints, coil U-bends, and flare fittings. Refrigerant oil often weeps at a leak and leaves an oily residue. Did you find any oily residue or obvious damage?
steps.expected_input: any oily residue, damage, or suspect joints found

branches.condition: Found an oily/visible suspect leak point

steps.title: Electronic leak detector sweep
steps.instruction: Using a calibrated electronic leak detector, sweep slowly (about 1 inch/second) along the low side first, then the high side, keeping the probe below the lines since most refrigerants are heavier than air. Did the detector alarm at a specific location?
steps.expected_input: whether the detector alarmed and where

branches.condition: Detector alarmed at a specific location
No detector hit anywhere on the system

steps.title: Confirm the leak with bubbles
steps.instruction: Confirm the suspected location with bubble solution — a steady stream of bubbles confirms the leak. Mark the confirmed leak location. Is the leak confirmed?

branches.condition: No bubbles — the earlier indication was a false positive

steps.title: Standing pressure test with dry nitrogen
steps.instruction: No leak found at operating pressure. Recover the refrigerant per EPA 608, then pressurize with dry nitrogen to the manufacturer's test pressure and hold a standing test. A pressure drop over the hold time confirms a leak; re-sweep with detector and bubbles to locate it.
steps.safety_note: Recover refrigerant per EPA 608 before introducing nitrogen. Never exceed the rated test pressure, and use a regulator.

branches.condition: Pressure dropped — leak confirmed, now locate it

steps.title: Document and repair the leak
steps.instruction: Photograph the confirmed leak location for the record. Recover refrigerant per EPA 608, repair the joint/component, pressure-test the repair, evacuate, and recharge to nameplate. Log the repair as the leak resolution.
steps.safety_note: Recover refrigerant per EPA 608 before opening the circuit to make the repair.

steps.title: No leak found
steps.instruction: Both operating-pressure and standing-nitrogen tests found no leak. The low charge may be from a prior service or a very slow leak below detection. Recommend documenting findings and escalating for extended monitoring or a longer standing test.
